{"query":"EU AI Act AI Omnibus annex III high-risk systems compliance deadline extension annex I regulated products deadline official text 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1814,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.gibsondunn.com/eu-ai-act-omnibus-agreement-postponed-high-risk-deadlines-and-other-key-changes/","title":"EU AI Act Omnibus Agreement — Postponed High-Risk Deadlines and Other Key Changes - Gibson Dunn","content":"The EU AI Act entered into force on 1 August 2024, establishing the world’s first comprehensive horizontal regulatory framework for artificial intelligence.
2 Aug 2027 — Deadline for member states to establish AI regulatory sandboxes (Article 57). — New — Annex I HRAIS obligations were originally due from this date but are proposed to be deferred to 2 Aug 2028."},{"url":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai","title":"AI Act | Shaping Europe’s digital future","content":"The AI Act entered into force on 1 August 2024, on 2 August 2026, This legislative proposal (dubbed as the 'AI omnibus') has been adopted on 19 November 2025 ...
The AI Act rules on GPAI became effective in August 2025.
prohibited AI practices and AI literacy obligations entered into application from 2 February 2025
the rules for high-risk AI systems - embedded into regulated products - have an extended transition period until 2 August 2028 (as a result of the political agreement on the proposal to simplify the AI Act – 'AI omnibus')
This legislative proposal (dubbed as the 'AI omnibus') has been adopted on 19 November 2025 and a political agreement was reached on 7 May 2026."},{"url":"https://www.lw.com/en/insights/ai-act-update-eu-resolves-to-change-rules-and-extend-deadlines","title":"AI Act Update: EU Resolves to Change Rules and Extend Deadlines","content":"This “AI Act Omnibus” forms part of the EU’s broader Omnibus legislative package aimed at simplifying digital regulation.
The extension of the HRAIS compliance deadlines is a significant change in practice.
- SME simplifications extended to mid-caps: The AI Act’s simplified compliance framework for small- and medium-sized enterprises will be extended to companies with up to 750 employees and €150 million in annual revenue.
The text will proceed to formal adoption by the European Parliament and the Council, which is expected by July 2026 — ahead of August 2, 2026, when HRAIS requirements would otherwise take effect.
Companies should reflect this evolving enforcement landscape in their AI Act compliance strategies and broader AI governance programs."},{"url":"https://www.winstontaylor.com/insights/ai-act-rules-on-high-risk-ai-delayed-as-ai-digital-omnibus-agreed","title":"AI Act rules on high-risk AI delayed as AI Digital Omnibus agreed - Winston Taylor","content":"On 19 November 2025, the European Commission published its Digital Omnibus proposal, the first part of which focuses on 'quick fixes' for some of the pain points in the AI Act.
It also provided a backstop in case no such Decision was made with the latest application dates set at 2 December 2027 (Annex III) and 2 August 2028 (Annex I).
New prohibited AI practice: a new prohibited AI practice relating to AI systems used for creation of non-consensual sexual and intimate images (NCII) and CSAM from 2 December 2026."},{"url":"https://www.questa-ai.com/privacy-cafe/eu-ai-act-countdown-is-your-annex-iii-system-ready-for-august-2026","title":"EU AI Act: Is Your Annex III System Ready for Compliance?","content":"Then, on May 7, 2026, EU lawmakers reached a provisional deal to push that deadline to December 2027.
Human Oversight (Article 14)
On May 7, 2026, the European Parliament, the Council of the EU, and the European Commission reached a political agreement on the "Digital Omnibus on AI" — the first amendment package to the AI Act since it became law.
If the agreement isn't published in the Official Journal before August 2, 2026, the original AI Act text applies exactly as written — Annex III high-risk obligations take effect on that date regardless of the political agreement.
High-risk system non-compliance can reach €15 million or 3% of global annual turnover.
See what Article 12 logging, Art."},{"url":"https://compliancehub.wiki/eu-ai-act-omnibus-high-risk-deadline-extension-compliance-2026/","title":"EU AI Act Omnibus Deal: High-Risk Deadlines Pushed to ...","content":"May 14, 2026 — August 2, 2026 — High-risk AI systems under Annex III applicable; August 2, 2027 — High-risk AI in regulated products under Annex I applicable.Read more"},{"url":"https://verifywise.ai/blog/eu-ai-act-omnibus-what-changed","title":"EU AI Act omnibus: the new high-risk deadlines explained","content":"May 14, 2026 — The EU AI Act omnibus pushed the big deadlines back: high-risk Annex III now applies from 2 December 2027, Annex I from 2 August 2028, ..."},{"url":"https://www.innovaiden.com/insights/eu-ai-act-august-deadline-moved-digital-omnibus","title":"The EU AI Act's August 2 High-Risk Deadline Just Moved. ...","content":"Jul 1, 2026 — High-risk obligations are deferred: stand-alone Annex III systems to 2 December 2027; AI embedded in Annex I regulated products to 2 August 2028 ...Read more"}]}

High-risk AI systems embedded in regulated products under Annex I","score":0.91936076,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.gibsondunn.com/eu-ai-act-omnibus-agreement-postponed-high-risk-deadlines-and-other-key-changes","title":"EU AI Act Omnibus Agreement — Postponed High-Risk Deadlines ...","content":"Its obligations roll out in staggered phases, with the most consequential provisions — covering high-risk AI systems (HRAIS) — originally set to apply from 2 August 2026 (for Annex III HRAIS) and 2 August 2027 (for Annex I HRAIS). After an initial round of trilogue negotiations on 28 April ended without success, the institutions returned to the table and reached a provisional political agreement on 6 May, subsequently confirmed by Member State representatives in the Council on 13 May. Formal adoption and publication in the Official Journal are expected in the coming weeks, in advance of the 2 August 2026 deadline. Under the proposal, high-risk AI system obligations under the AI Act will be postponed by over a year compared to the original timeline: stand-alone Annex III systems (covering, among others, recruitment, credit scoring, law enforcement, education, and border control tools) will now need to comply by **2 December 2027**, and AI embedded in regulated products under Annex I — such as medical devices, machinery, and vehicles — by **2 August 2028**.","score":0.9100432,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.insideglobaltech.com/2026/05/28/eu-ai-act-update-timeline-relief-targeted-simplification-and-new-prohibitions","title":"EU AI Act Update: Timeline Relief, Targeted Simplification, ...","content":"On 7 May 2026, negotiators from the Council of the European Union, the European Parliament, and the European Commission reached a provisional agreement on the terms of the Digital Omnibus on AI, marking the first set of amendments to the EU AI Act since its adoption in June 2024. * Annex III HRAIS (use-based): Obligations are postponed from 2 August 2026 to 2 December 2027 (i.e., deferred by 16 months). Transparency obligations under Article 50(2): For AI systems generating or manipulating synthetic content and placed on the EU market or put into service before 2 August 2026, the provider’s obligation to ensure that the system’s outputs are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated is postponed from 2 August 2026 to 2 December 2026 (i.e., deferred by 4 months). Now that the Machinery Regulation is listed under Section B, the full suite of Chapter III, AI Act obligations will no longer apply directly to such products; instead, the Commission must adopt delegated acts amending Annex III of the Machinery Regulation to incorporate AI-specific health and safety requirements by 2 August 2028.","score":0.82391036,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://airia.com/blog/the-eu-ai-act-just-got-an-extension-heres-how-to-use-it","title":"The EU AI Act Just Got An Extension. Here's How to Use It.","content":"Here’s How to Use It. The EU AI Act's Digital Omnibus shifts deadlines — but compliance requirements haven't changed. Here's what to do with your 16 months. The EU’s Digital Omnibus provisional agreement delays Annex III high-risk AI compliance to December 2, 2027 — but this is not a reset. What’s critical to act on now:. Use the 16-month window to build operational governance — complete your AI inventory, run risk classifications, establish audit trails, and wire in human oversight — before the deadline arrives. On May 7, 2026, the EU Council and European Parliament reached a provisional agreement to delay and simplify parts of the EU AI Act. For AI governance professionals, this has triggered two opposite reactions: relief that an impossible August deadline has moved, and anxiety about what “moved” actually means for programs already in flight. What the Omnibus has given you is 16 months to do this properly with the right tooling, the right inventory, and governance built into your operations rather than bolted on at the last minute.","score":0.8106142,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/annex/3","title":"Annex III: High-Risk AI Systems Referred to in Article 6(2) | EU Artificial Intelligence Act","content":"BETA **🔔 New: Try the new AI Act Explorer, rebuilt from the ground up for 2026.** Read the official text in all 24 EU languages, including the 'Code of Practice' and other documents. Chapter III: High-Risk AI System. Section 1: Classification of AI Systems as High-Risk. Article 6: Classification Rules for High-Risk AI Systems. Section 2: Requirements for High-Risk AI Systems. Section 3: Obligations of Providers and Deployers of High-Risk AI Systems and Other Parties. Article 16: Obligations of Providers of High-Risk AI Systems. Article 22: Authorised Representatives of Providers of High-Risk AI Systems. Article 26: Obligations of Deployers of High-Risk AI Systems. Chapter IV: Transparency Obligations for Providers and Deployers of Certain AI Systems. Section 2: Obligations for Providers of General-Purpose AI Models. Article 53: Obligations for Providers of General-Purpose AI Models. Section 3: Obligations of Providers of General-Purpose AI Models with Systemic Risk. Article 55: Obligations for Providers of General-Purpose AI Models with Systemic Risk.","score":0.8055255,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":2.6,"request_id":"e1325029-6dca-4081-9df4-0e1a2e803368"}
